name: 750ispossible,LLC
description_short: 750ispossible, LLC is a credit repair company that disputes negative items on credit reports to improve client scores. Based in Everett, WA, they charge from $99/month with no long-term contracts.
description_long:
750ispossible, LLC operates as a credit repair service based in Everett, Washington, founded around 2017. The company specializes in challenging negative items on credit reports through direct work with credit bureaus and creditors, positioning itself as an alternative to larger national credit repair firms.

The company offers credit repair services including: free credit consultations and analysis, in-depth credit report review (for $1), ongoing dispute filing with credit agencies, real-time account access for progress tracking, and student loan rehabilitation services for defaulted loans. They also provide credit education materials (including a book available on Amazon) and a referral program. Their business model centers on monthly fees starting at $99 with no binding contracts and a money-back guarantee.

What distinguishes 750ispossible is their emphasis on personalized service through a named principal (Phil), competitive low pricing without hidden fees, and rapid claimed results (one testimonial cites score improvements in less than 3 months). They market a "money back guarantee" and position themselves as more effective than larger competitors like Lexington Law based on client testimonials.

However, significant caveats exist. The website makes broad claims like "$6.9M+ deleted from reports" without substantiation or context about what constitutes a "deletion." Testimonials, while detailed, are unverified. The $1 credit pull fee and focus on credit repair—a service category with ongoing regulatory scrutiny—means consumers should understand that legitimate disputes take time and that results are not guaranteed despite marketing language. No information is provided about licensing, CROA compliance details, or dispute methodology specifics.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Low starting price of $99/month with no long-term binding contracts
No hidden fees explicitly stated on pricing page
Free initial consultation and credit analysis before enrollment
24/7 client portal access for real-time progress tracking on disputes
Money-back guarantee if results are not achieved
Handles specialized services including student loan default rehabilitation
Claims rapid results with one client reportedly improving credit in weeks vs. months with competitors
cons: Unsubstantiated claim of '$6,963,691 deleted from reports' with no breakdown of what constitutes a deletion or verification method
Testimonials are unverified and may not reflect typical consumer outcomes
No visible information about CROA compliance, licensing, or dispute process details on the website
Comparison claims against 'Lexington Law' based on single testimonial rather than documented performance data
Credit repair inherently requires months to show results despite marketing emphasis on quick turnarounds
best_for: Consumers with negative items on credit reports seeking personalized, local credit repair support
Borrowers with student loans in default looking for rehabilitation services
People who prefer working with a small, local service provider over large national credit repair companies
diagnosis: 750ispossible is best for consumers seeking personalized, low-cost credit repair with claimed rapid results and local service from a small provider. The primary caveat is that credit repair claims require independent verification, testimonials are unverified, and meaningful score improvement from legitimate disputes typically takes several months despite any marketing messaging suggesting faster timelines.
services: In-depth credit report review and pulling ($1 fee)
Credit dispute filing with credit bureaus and creditors
Real-time account monitoring and progress updates
Client portal access for 24/7 account management
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
